docs.title: Synthesize
docs.text:
flows:
  default:
    nodes:
      tests:
        target: tests
        trigger: code-changes
      types:
        target: types
        trigger: code-changes
      docs:
        target: docs
        trigger:
          type: restart

targets:
  tests:
    commands: |
      pytest -vv --cov

  types:
    commands: |
      mypy

  docs:
    commands: |
      mkdocs serve --strict

triggers:
  code-changes:
    type: watch
    paths:
      - synthesize/
      - tests/
      - docs/examples/
      - pyproject.toml
      - .coveragerc

flowchart TD
  tests(tests)
  w_71023567e45aa5ff4e982e721dcdbe40[("synthesize/
tests/
docs/examples/
pyproject.toml
.coveragerc")]
  w_71023567e45aa5ff4e982e721dcdbe40 -->|👁| tests
  types(types)
  w_71023567e45aa5ff4e982e721dcdbe40 -->|👁| types
  docs(docs)
  docs -->|∞ 1s| docs

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• #3 Added PyPI classifiers and other metadata.
  • #33 #40 Allow injecting arguments (via Jinja2 templates) and environment variables into target commands. Arguments and environment variables can be specified at either the flow, node, or target level, with the most specific taking precedence.
  • #43 Mermaid diagrams can be generated for a flow using the --mermaid option.

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• #30 Reorganized configuration to separate targets, triggers (formerly "lifecycles"), and flows (graphs of targets and triggers)."
  • #41 Execution duration is printed in the completion message.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text: • #45 Restart triggers now allow for the node's children to run again after the node completes.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• #1 Core graph-of-targets data model and executor, with support for once, restart, and watch lifecycles.
  • #1 Support for YAML configuration files.

docs.title: Once
docs.text:
"Once" triggers run the node just one time during the flow. This is the default trigger.

Use this trigger when a command needs to run only one time during a flow.

flows:
  default:
    nodes:
      A:
        target: sleep-and-echo
      B:
        target: sleep-and-echo
      C:
        target: sleep-and-echo

targets:
  sleep-and-echo:
    commands: |
      sleep 1
      echo "Hi from {{ id }}!"

flowchart TD
  A(A)
  B(B)
  C(C)

docs.title: After
docs.text:
"After" triggers run the node after some other nodes have completed.

Use this trigger when a node depends on the output of another node.

flows:
  default:
    nodes:
      A:
        target: sleep-and-echo
      B:
        target: sleep-and-echo
      C:
        target: sleep-and-echo
      D:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          after: ["A", "B"]
      E:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          after: ["C"]
      F:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          after: ["D", "E"]

targets:
  sleep-and-echo:
    commands: |
      sleep 1
      echo "Hi from {{ id }}!"

flowchart TD
  A(A)
  B(B)
  C(C)
  D(D)
  A --> D
  B --> D
  E(E)
  C --> E
  F(F)
  D --> F
  E --> F

docs.title: Restart
docs.text:
"Restart" triggers run the node every time the node is completed.

Use this trigger when you want to keep the node's command running.

flows:
  default:
    nodes:
      A:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          type: restart
          delay: 3
      B:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          type: restart
          delay: 1

targets:
  sleep-and-echo:
    commands: |
      sleep 1
      echo "Hi from {{ id }}!"

flowchart TD
  A(A)
  A -->|∞ 3s| A
  B(B)
  B -->|∞ 1s| B

docs.title: Watch
docs.text:
"Watch" triggers run the node every time one of the watched files changes (directories are watched recursively).

Use this trigger to run a node in reaction to changes in the filesystem.

flows:
  default:
    nodes:
      A:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          type: watch
          paths: ["synthesize/", "tests/"]
      B:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          type: watch
          paths: [ "docs/" ]

targets:
  sleep-and-echo:
    commands: |
      sleep 1
      echo "Hi from {{ id }}!"

flowchart TD
  A(A)
  w_e00c7e8478b60649551a53dd05f75842[("synthesize/
tests/")]
  w_e00c7e8478b60649551a53dd05f75842 -->|👁| A
  B(B)
  w_49e56c817e5e54854c35e136979f97ca[("docs/")]
  w_49e56c817e5e54854c35e136979f97ca -->|👁| B

docs.title: Restart + After
docs.text:
flows:
  default:
    nodes:
      A:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          type: restart
          delay: 10
      B:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          after: ["A"]
      C:
        target: sleep-and-echo
        trigger:
          after: ["B"]

targets:
  sleep-and-echo:
    commands: |
      sleep 2
      echo "Hi from {{ id }}!"

flowchart TD
  A(A)
  A -->|∞ 10s| A
  B(B)
  A --> B
  C(C)
  B --> C
